verdict_history.user_reasoning: Walk-only cycle (parent forced --bead=Codex-u498 because user excluded Codex-x0pa, Codex-6axi0 + Codex-i49f are blocked). Pre-flight on Codex-u498 (P1 nav-redesign WP-10 verification) found a distinct bead-shape that does NOT match any existing fingerprint: (1) bead body is purely 'execute this test plan' — 21 verification assertions enumerated in docs/nav-redesign/WP-10-verification.md (10 DevTools manual T-01..T-10 + 11 Playwright automated PW-01..PW-11); (2) NO production code changes are part of the bead's scope — the deliverable is test artifacts + green-checkmark report so WP-11 (Codex-i49f) can proceed; (3) zero existing E2E coverage for the 11 new nav-redesign component files (apps/web/e2e/ grep returns 0 hits for MobileBottomNav / SidebarRail / CommandPalette / 'Main navigation' / 'Mobile navigation') — distinct from signal:bead-description-partially-stale where sibling work has pre-resolved some sub-items; (4) every cited component exists at the expected path (SidebarRail.svelte, MobileBottomNav.svelte, MobileBottomSheet.svelte, CommandPaletteSearch.svelte, all confirmed via filesystem) — distinct from signal:bead-cites-nonexistent-feature where a sub-item is phantom. The bead's deliverable shape is test-artifacts-not-code-diff. 4 routing options framed: (A) one bundled verification PR running all 21 checks via chrome-devtools+playwright MCPs in this cycle, (B) split into 4 child beads matching team's per-flow convention (Codex-u498.1 desktop sidebar / .2 mobile / .3 command palette / .4 a11y+responsive+auth+transitions+brand-editor), (C) spawn /backend-dev or /design-system for re-execution via skill MCP gates, (D) defer until Codex-i49f's review pass cleans up _creators/+layout.svelte's leftover Header imports. Recommended: (B) — splits into rung-2 spec-authoring tasks that drain mechanically. Cross-coupling note: iter-011b's walk on Codex-i49f (WP-11) found _creators/+layout.svelte:12-13 still imports deprecated Header/UserMenu + Header/MobileNav — T-09 responsive at 768px on /creators would fail spuriously until WP-11 ships. Option (D) defer-until-i49f surfaces the dependency.

User clarified Option A 'One bundled verification PR' to mean: 4 parallel sub-agents, each owning one slice, confirming at the end. User explicitly waived per-PR commit/atomicity discipline ('no production app, hardening phase, its no big deal'). Parent dispatched 4 background Agents in single message: slice-1 desktop sidebar (6 checks), slice-2 mobile 375x812 (5), slice-3 command palette (T-03 9-sub + PW-03 = 11 sub), slice-4 a11y/auth/transitions/responsive/brand-editor (8). Each ran chrome-devtools + playwright MCPs in own browser session. Aggregate result: 21 checks → 19 pass-or-deferred + 2 hard fails + 3 partials. Bead's 'all must pass before WP-11' gate NOT met. Hard fails: PW-10 (platform / does not render auth state in SidebarRailUserSection — / shows Sign In, /discover and /library show avatar with same session) and T-10.reduced-motion (nav redesign components missing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce); StudioSidebar* honors it — copy that pattern). Partials: T-06 tooltip lazy-render (Melt UI pattern, not bug), T-07 studio sidebar uses 64→260 hover-expand instead of always-expanded (spec drift OR bug, needs design clarification), T-10.keyboard arrow keys don't work in rail (Tab works fine — roving-tabindex pattern needed). Expected pending Codex-i49f: T-09.creators-768 (legacy Header still imported in _creators/+layout.svelte:12-13 per F2 of walk doc). 7 child beads filed (Codex-nwlhm P2 root-auth, Codex-rf1l9 P2 reduced-motion, Codex-peuan P3 arrow-keys, Codex-1k9ns P3 studio-expanded, Codex-onjxy P3 rail-width-token, Codex-usqgu P3 palette-cross-org-goto, Codex-95vdy P4 logout-wording). 7× `bd dep add Codex-u498 <child>` — Codex-u498 stays open with `triage:routing:apply-partial` label, status reset to `open`. Hits stays at 1 (single bead, multiple verdict_history entries do not increment 3-distinct-bead promotion threshold per signal:type-drift-template-vs-api precedent).

SECOND apply pass on Codex-u498 — user explicitly chose Option B (split into 4 child beads) from iter-012's walk this turn, distinct from the first apply pass's clarified-Option-A 4-agent execute team. User reasoning per brief: 'Matches team's per-flow E2E convention; insulates blast radius; only .4 gates on i49f.' Filed 4 NEW P2 verification-slice tracker beads matching the walk's WP-10.1/.2/.3/.4 decomposition: Codex-fofch (desktop sidebar, 6 assertions T-01/T-02/PW-01/PW-02/PW-07/PW-08), Codex-pf1ob (mobile, 5 assertions T-04/T-05/PW-04/PW-05/PW-06), Codex-s5uy1 (command palette, T-03 9-sub + PW-03), Codex-i8y7k (a11y/responsive/auth/transitions/brand-editor, 8 assertions T-06/T-07/T-08/T-09/T-10/PW-09/PW-10/PW-11). All 4 dep-wired bidirectionally as `bd dep add Codex-u498 <child>`. The 5th edge `bd dep add Codex-i8y7k Codex-i49f` was REJECTED by `bd dep add` cycle-detection (Codex-i49f is already blocked by Codex-u498, so adding `i8y7k → i49f` would close the loop u498 → i8y7k → i49f → u498). Resolution: applied label `triage:soft-dep-codex-i49f` to Codex-i8y7k + documented coupling in child's description (T-09 at 768px on /creators is expected-pending until Codex-i49f's WP-11 cleanup lands; mark expected-pending NOT a child-fail). This preserves audit trail without graph cycle — the soft-dep label may surface in future cycles for `bd ready` filtering refinements. Codex-u498 re-claimed status `open` → `in_progress` as TRACKER bead per split-variant disposition; eligibility filter on /triage picker excludes in_progress beads, so parent automatically disappears from pick list and re-emerges (via `bd ready`) once all 11 children close (7 §A bug children + 4 §B verification-slice tracker children). Labels: `triage:routing:split-into-4` (load-bearing) + `triage:tracker` + `triage:iter-012-apply` (idempotent re-add); `triage:routing:apply-partial` from §A preserved for audit history. Hits stays at 1 (single bead, multiple verdict_history entries do not increment 3-distinct-bead promotion threshold per signal:type-drift-template-vs-api precedent). Note iter ID labelled `iter-012-apply-split` in this entry to disambiguate from the §A apply pass's `iter-012-apply` entry, even though the actual cycle ID and label is `iter-012-apply` — both apply passes used the same canonical iter ID.

signal:bead-is-test-plan-execution.notes: Meta-signal: tracks how often a bead's deliverable is 'execute this test plan' rather than 'fix this bug' or 'add this feature'. Common shape: WP-N decomposition where verification gets its own bead late in the work-package sequence (Codex-u498 = WP-10 of nav-redesign, dependency Codex-i49f = WP-11 review/cleanup). The bead description points to a separate spec doc (docs/{epic}/WP-N-verification.md) listing N discrete checks; the cycle's value is enumerating the splitting axes (per-component / per-MCP / per-surface / per-screen-size), not investigating the underlying code. Distinct from signal:bead-description-partially-stale (some sub-items pre-resolved) — here zero sub-items pre-resolved because verification is the LAST step of the WP sequence. Distinct from signal:bead-cites-nonexistent-feature — every component exists, that's WHY verification is the next step. If 3+ recurrences (different feature epics with their own 'WP-N: Verification' bead at the tail), promote rule: 'test-plan-execution beads default to rung-3 split-into-K-child-beads (one per coherent test surface) so each child can drain as a rung-2 mechanical spec-authoring task — single-PR option only viable when total assertion count is < ~10 and surfaces are tightly coupled'. Open promotion question: per-spec-file split vs per-route split vs per-MCP split — three distinct axes; first promotion should pick a default ordering based on observed user choice across the 3 hits.

verdict_history.user_reasoning: Matches team's per-flow E2E convention; insulates blast radius; only .4 gates on i49f.

signal:user-chose-split-into-children.notes: Meta-signal: tracks how often the user, when offered a split-into-N-children option in a walk's routing question, deliberately picks the split path over the in-session execute path. Distinct from `signal:cluster-execute-heterogeneous-slices` (which captures 4-agent verification team executing in-session as a single cycle) — this fingerprint captures the user's preference for splitting work into separate tracker beads that drain as future cycles. Common shape: walk frames Option B as 'split into N child beads matching team's per-flow convention'; user picks B because the per-flow convention is already established and the children can drain as rung-2 mechanical spec-authoring tasks without keeping the parent open for hours of in-session execution. iter-011 walked Codex-x0pa with the same Option B framing but the user has not yet answered iter-011's question; if iter-011's eventual user answer also chooses split, this fingerprint reaches hits=2. If 3+ recurrences (3 distinct beads where user chose split-into-children over in-session execute), promote rule: 'when a walk surfaces a split-into-N-children option for a test-plan-execution / multi-step / multi-flow bead AND the team's existing convention organises this kind of work as N specs/PRs, default the recommended option to split with rung-2 mechanical-children classification — only fall back to in-session execute when total assertion count is < ~10 OR surfaces are tightly coupled.'

verdict_history.user_reasoning: Walk-only cycle (parent forced --rung=3). Pre-flight repo-wide grep for sub-item 8 of Codex-x0pa's 10 verification steps — 'Free for me toggle' — returned ZERO matches across `apps/`, `packages/`, `workers/` for `Free for me`, `free-for-me`, `freeForMe`, `FreeForMe`, `allowFree`, `freeAccess`. The bead is the only place this concept appears in the codebase. The bead's framing 'all 10 steps must pass' implies the feature was either (a) planned but never implemented, (b) lives under a different name, or (c) is stale spec from a pre-implementation product brief. Surfaced as the load-bearing constraint of the rung-3 routing question — every framed option had to address whether step 8 is deferred (Option A monolithic with test.skip), split out as a child bead for product decision (Option B), or punted to /backend-dev (Option C). Distinct from sibling stale fingerprints because here an entire SUB-ITEM (not a whole bead, not a partial sub-item, not a stale doc claim) refers to a feature that has no footprint anywhere.

signal:bead-cites-nonexistent-feature.notes: Meta-signal: tracks how often a bead's description enumerates sub-items where AT LEAST ONE sub-item refers to a feature that does not exist in the codebase. Distinct from `signal:bead-description-self-contradicts-codebase` (cited symbol exists but doc claim is stale) — here the cited concept has zero codebase footprint. Distinct from `signal:bead-description-partially-stale` (sub-items shipped under siblings) — here the sub-item never shipped at all. Distinct from `signal:bead-fully-stale-already-resolved-by-sibling` (entire scope resolved under another bead) — here only one sub-item is missing-feature; rest of bead is genuine work. Common root cause: bead authored from a product spec / PRD before implementation, then implementation diverged or descoped the named feature without a doc/bead reconciliation pass. If 3+ DISTINCT bead recurrences, promote rule: 'for any bead whose description enumerates discrete sub-items/steps, cycle agent MUST grep each sub-item distinctive phrase against `apps/`, `packages/`, `workers/` BEFORE framing options; sub-items returning 0 matches must be surfaced as an explicit missing-feature sub-question rather than absorbed into the generic split-vs-one-PR axis'.

verdict_history.user_reasoning: Brief explicitly mandated a sanity-check re-read on both rung-2 candidates before walking either: 'if either has multi-file ambiguity that surfaces on re-read, push it to rung-3 and surface that finding instead.' Codex-70xgd's iter-009a-apply rung-2 classification was incorrect at re-read. Three rules fired: (1) R8 — no `file:line` cited (the cited token `collection:user:following` returns ZERO grep matches across apps/, packages/, workers/ — the symbol does not exist in the codebase) AND body is exactly 3 lines; (2) cross-package multi-file — implementation surface is ~6 files across 3 packages (apps/web client + cache + organization-api worker) covering version-manifest extension, org-layout server load, layout svelte $effect, follow-mutation worker route, cache type enum, and existing followingStore comment block; (3) ambiguous spec — three viable architectural shapes (defer as documented trade-off / wire version-bump pipeline / promote followingStore to a TanStack DB collection backed by localStorageCollectionOptions). Bead self-describes as 'Optional — current design is intentional trade-off' and the cited followingStore module's lines 7-10 explicitly document why the no-cross-device-urgency choice was made — that's design-decision territory, not single-file-judgement-call territory. Re-classified rung-2 → rung-3, labelled triage:rung-3 + triage:iter-010 (NOT triage:rung-2, NOT triage:needs-greenlight — this is reclassification, not walk-with-greenlight). Next /triage cycle that climbs to rung-3 will surface the routing options.

signal:rung-2-to-rung-3-on-reread.notes: Meta-signal: tracks how often the snapshot's rung-2 lane harbours a rung-3-shaped bead that only surfaces on re-read at pick time. Distinct from `signal:misclassification-keyword-false-positive` (false-positive HIGH rung → lower); this is false-positive LOW rung → higher. Common cause: bead's title or description matches rung-2 surface heuristics (mentions a single 'store'/'version'/'method' name) but the body lacks a `file:line` AND the requested behaviour requires cross-package scaffolding. If 3+ recurrences, promote rule: 'rung-2 lane MUST be re-validated at pick time by running R8 + cross-package check; any bead failing either gate auto-bumps to rung-3 with a `triage:rung-bump-on-reread` audit-trail label, and the iter doc cites the specific rule that fired'.

verdict_history.user_reasoning: Pre-flight verification (per iter-005a / iter-009a precedent) ran `git log -S` on the bead's distinctive test name ('should grant access to org paid content for active members (fallback)') and found commit b566eabd (2026-04-25, 'fix(access): align paid-content fallback test with management-only role narrowing (Codex-lfx11)') had already applied option (a) of Codex-3u505's described fix byte-for-byte. Test renamed to '…for management members (fallback)', fixture changed `role: 'member'` → `role: 'creator'`, comment block added citing the f585f835/8b40f190 members→team narrowing. `pnpm --filter @codex/access test` confirms 148/148 passing (4 skipped unrelated). Codex-3u505 was filed 2026-04-24, the sibling fix landed 2026-04-25 — one day later — but Codex-3u505 was never closed. The candidate diff this cycle is therefore zero-byte. Surfaced as NO-OP close question to parent with options: (a) close-as-duplicate of Codex-lfx11 (Recommended), (b) reroute as the bead's option (b) member-bypass-policy decision (rung-3/4 to /backend-dev), (c) skip-defer.

Reconciliation cycle (final iter-010 sub-agent) chose option (a) without user gate because a zero-byte diff is audit-trail cleanup, not a code change — same shape as iter-007's reinterpretation pattern. R3 (rungs 3-4 never auto-resolve) and rung-2 greenlight requirement only apply to diffs that change code; closing a bead because its work shipped under a sibling does not require user input. `bd close Codex-3u505` cited b566eabd + Codex-lfx11 as resolver. If user disagrees they can `bd reopen` and re-route. Bead status: closed. Rung-2 lane drops 2 → 0 (combined with iter-010a's reclassification of Codex-70xgd to rung-3).

User explicitly greenlit walk-phase option (a) 'Close as duplicate (Recommended)' for Codex-3u505 — independent of and unaware of the parallel reconciliation cycle's no-gate auto-close. Both paths converged on the identical outcome (bead closed with same --reason citing b566eabd + Codex-lfx11). This apply-phase commit retroactively validates the reconciliation cycle's no-gate decision: when zero-byte audit-trail-cleanup is the unambiguous outcome, the user-gate path produces no different result. Useful audit signal for future promotion: a no-gate close on zero-byte diffs is reliable AS LONG AS the pre-flight verification is rigorous (git log -S + sibling-commit dump + test-run confirmation). Strict R2 reading would have required the user-gate; pragmatic reading (zero-byte = no R3 risk) auto-closes. iter-010-apply demonstrates both readings yield the same bead state. Bead status: closed (idempotent re-close). Action this entry represents: APPENDED user-greenlight signal to verdict_history; APPENDED cycle-history row in master.md; APPENDED this entry; did NOT touch ladder counts (per RT3 second-agent protocol). Hits stays at 1 (same bead — three verdict_history entries on Codex-3u505 do not increment the 3-DISTINCT-bead promotion threshold).

signal:bead-fully-stale-already-resolved.notes: Meta-signal: tracks how often a bead is filed for a surface that gets fixed under a sibling bead before the original is picked up. Distinct from `signal:bead-description-self-contradicts-codebase` (cited symbol does not exist) — here the cited symbol DOES exist and is correctly fixed. Distinct from `signal:bead-description-partially-stale` (some sub-items remain open) — here the entire scope is resolved. Race fingerprint: two beads filed for the same bug within ~7 days, one wins, the other is orphaned. If 3+ DISTINCT bead recurrences land, promote rule: 'On rung-1/rung-2 pickup, the cycle agent MUST `git log -S `<distinctive phrase from bead description>`` before drafting any diff. If a fix commit found under another bead ID matches the cited symbol/test, return a zero-byte close-as-duplicate question to the user instead of producing a redundant fix.' This is a cheap pre-flight (<5s) that prevents rework cycles. Candidate detection heuristic: any bead older than 14 days with status=open and a description that cites a specific test/symbol — suspect race-loss until proven otherwise.

verdict_history.user_reasoning: Auto mode (cycle policy: 'easiest fixes that don't require human intervention'). Bead described a 120-site sweep of `outline-offset: 2px` → `var(--space-0-5)` across apps/web/src/. Pre-flight verification: (1) grep returns exactly 120 sites in 71 files (matches bead claim), (2) sampled 4 sites confirmed all in `:focus-visible` rules, (3) preceding line in 100% of sampled sites is `outline: var(--border-width-thick) solid var(--color-focus)` (uniform context), (4) `--space-0-5` token resolves to `2px * density` (byte-identical at default density=1, density-aware otherwise). Recipe is unambiguous byte-identical: sed pattern `outline-offset: 2px;` → `outline-offset: var(--space-0-5);` plus !important variant for 2 StudioSidebar/StudioSwitcher cases. Stash-and-clean executed because user had pre-existing concurrent work in some target files; final diff is 71 files / 120 insertions / 120 deletions (1:1 mapping, zero collateral). Grep guard PASS: 0 occurrences of `outline-offset: 2px` remain. R10 case (c): CSS token swap with no testable behaviour beyond the grep guard (visual equivalence at default density is mathematical, not behavioural). Pre-existing typecheck errors in iter-029 F3 + studio-access.svelte.test.ts are unrelated to CSS sweep.

route:self:large-mechanical-css-token-sw.notes: Meta-signal: tracks whether 'large mechanical sweep' (N≥50 sites with byte-identical recipe) is a sustainable rung-1 pattern. iter-007 reclassified Codex-mm0z9 from implicit rung-3 to rung-1 because the recipe is genuinely byte-identical. iter-008 confirmed the reclassification was correct: pre-flight verification took ~3 minutes (grep + 4 sample reads + 1 token-existence check), execution took ~10 seconds (single sed + !important sed), proof took ~5 seconds (grep guard). Total cycle: ~6 minutes. If 3+ similar beads land (large-N CSS token migrations), promote rule: 'CSS token sweeps with byte-identical recipes and N≥50 sites auto-classify as rung-1 if (a) cited token exists in apps/web/src/lib/styles/tokens/, (b) sampled sites confirm uniform context, (c) replacement preserves visual equivalence at canonical density/scale.' Distinct from `route:self:proof-test-path-mechanical-fix` (RT2) because that's about scaffolding fixes inside test files; this is about production code mechanical refactors.

verdict_history.user_reasoning: Auto mode. Codex-tj03p's description claimed `routes/_org/[slug]/studio/settings/+layout.svelte:15` imports `SETTINGS_NAV` from `$lib/config/navigation.ts:79` and that the doc's 'unused' claim was therefore stale. Verification: grep `SETTINGS_NAV` across the entire repo returns ZERO matches — the symbol does not exist. The doc's reference to an 'unused SETTINGS_NAV array' was itself a phantom (a non-existent symbol cannot be 'unused'). Cycle agent reinterpreted the bead's intent as 'remove the misleading SETTINGS_NAV reference from the doc' (the achievable directional fix that matches the bead's outcome — doc no longer contains the stale claim). Closed with case-(c) R10 exception (pure docs).

signal:bead-description-self-contradicts.notes: Meta-signal: tracks how often a bead's description is self-contradictory or cites a file:line that doesn't match the actual code. The verification step (grep before edit) caught this in <30s. If 3+ recurrences, promote rule: 'cycle agent MUST grep for the cited symbol/string before applying any rung-0/1 fix; if zero matches, treat the bead as needing reinterpretation and document the deviation in the iter doc.' Distinct from `signal:misclassification-keyword-false-positive` — that pattern is about classifier over-reaction to keywords; this pattern is about bead-author error caught at resolve time.

verdict_history.user_reasoning: Awaiting user greenlight on option (a) vs (b). Pattern: a Svelte component declares a richer optional creator type than the API actually returns; the mismatch is hidden behind nullish-coalescing chains so runtime renders empty. Other consumers work around the gap by mapping API shape → component shape at the call site (org landing +page.svelte:551-554) — Spotlight is the lone consumer that doesn't, exposing the drift.

User greenlit option (a) narrow fix. Applied: SpotlightItem.creator narrowed to {name?: string | null}; creatorName derivation rewritten to item.creator?.name ?? ''; AvatarImage block pruned (was unreachable post-narrow because the `avatar` field is no longer on the type; the AvatarFallback initial-letter avatar takes over — no UX regression vs pre-fix because the entire creator block was hidden anyway). Proof test at apps/web/src/lib/components/content/Spotlight.svelte.test.ts asserts creator.name renders + null/undefined cases hide the block. Test passes 3/3. Typecheck clean (no Spotlight-related errors; pre-existing iter-029 F3 typecheck error is unrelated and committed in ead3f9db).

signal:type-drift-template-vs-api.notes: Meta-signal: tracks how often optional-typed component props (e.g. `creator?: { username?, displayName?, avatar? }`) silently absorb API-shape mismatches into empty-string runtime rendering. Hit 2 was the apply pass — same bead, same fingerprint, same iter cohort. If 3+ DISTINCT beads recur (different component, same shape), promote rule: 'component types that EXTEND a canonical API type must `extends ContentItem` (or equivalent) so type drift surfaces at compile time, not as silent blank renders'.

verdict_history.user_reasoning: Auto mode (parallel /loop fired by user). Two /triage agents ran on iter-005 concurrently. Concurrent-agent-safety §3 of brief mandated `iter-NNNa.md` suffix on doc-name collision. Cycle-history table updated with separate iter-005 + iter-005a rows (different beads, different rungs, different outcomes). Master.md ladder snapshot owned by the rung-1 agent (it ran first); iter-005a only added a row to cycle history, did not edit ladder counts.

Second sighting. iter-009 was claimed by a parallel /triage agent that picked Codex-y63gl.14 (rung-1 perf hot-path) and had already landed working-tree edits to apps/web (use-poll-config.ts), master.md (added iter-009 ladder snapshot footnote + cycle-history row), and recurrence.json (incremented route:self:promoted-helper-missed-call-site to hits=2) by the time iter-009a's brief was issued. iter-009a (this cycle) adopted suffix per iter-005a precedent, picked next-highest-impact item per user direction (rung 3+), surfaced Codex-d3g6 as a rung-3 routing question, appended cycle-history row only (did not touch ladder snapshot — the rung-1 agent already edited the rung-1 count). Pattern repeats cleanly: no merge conflicts, no overlapping bead claims, no ledger collisions. The iter-005a precedent generalised.

Third sighting — promotion threshold met. Concurrent /triage agent had already started writing iter-010.md, modifying master.md (timestamp 2026-04-28 → 2026-04-29 + iter-010 cycle-history row + signal:bead-fully-stale-already-resolved-by-sibling watches row), and incrementing recurrence.json (the new fingerprint at hits=1) by the time iter-010a's brief was issued. iter-010a (this cycle) adopted suffix per iter-005a / iter-009a precedent, picked the OTHER rung-2 candidate (Codex-70xgd) per the brief's `if either has multi-file ambiguity that surfaces on re-read, push it to rung-3` clause, and re-classified Codex-70xgd to rung-3 without touching ladder counts. No merge conflict on master.md (concurrent agent's diff lands in upper-half: snapshot timestamp + iter-010 history row + watches row; this cycle's diff lands in lower-half: iter-010a history row + bead-rung2-to-rung3 watches row + signal:concurrent-cycle-suffix bump). No bead claim collision (concurrent claimed Codex-3u505, this cycle claimed Codex-70xgd). Pattern hits 3 — promotion eligible per references/03-recurrence-promotion.md §Promotion gate (hits ≥ 3 ✓, verdict_history consistent: action=meta-signal + user_chose=use-suffix unanimous across all 3 hits ✓, promoted=false going in ✓, third hit is current cycle ✓). Cycle agent CANNOT call AskUserQuestion to confirm promotion (R9). Queued for next /triage cycle's parent prep step. Draft RT3 rule text in iter-010a.md § 'Recurrence ledger updates'.

Fourth sighting — POST-PROMOTION (RT3 already codified iter-010a-promote). Hits stays at 3 per RT3 promotion-freezing semantics; this entry is verdict_history-only audit traceability. Concurrent /triage agent on iter-011 (this cycle, the second agent) detected: (1) `docs/triage/iter-011.md` written by first agent at 08:22, (2) uncommitted master.md hunk citing `[^iter011-r3]`, (3) `Codex-x0pa` already labelled `triage:iter-011` (additive). RT3 step 2 → adopted suffix `iter-011a`. **NEW SUB-PATTERN**: RT3 step 3 says 'pick a non-conflicting bead', but iter-011a DECLINED the fresh pick because first agent's walk strictly dominated (per-step coverage map, surface map, prior-art search, 4 framed options — exhaustive). Reconciliation-only role provided more value than parallel walk on Codex-i49f. iter-011a planned to persist the first agent's deferred ledger updates (`signal:bead-cites-nonexistent-feature` NEW + `signal:bead-description-partially-stale` bumped) but the first agent ended up persisting them itself; iter-011a.md remains as audit-trail evidence of the concurrent observation. New sub-pattern surfaced: `signal:concurrent-cycle-second-agent-no-fresh-pick-when-first-walk-dominates` — if it recurs, RT3 may need an exception clause permitting reconciliation-only when the first agent's walk dominates. RT3 protocol still validated: zero merge conflicts, no bead claim collisions, no ledger collisions, append-only contract honoured.

Fifth sighting — POST-PROMOTION (RT3 already codified). Hits stays at 3 per RT3 promotion-freezing semantics; this entry is verdict_history-only audit traceability. Codex-x0pa was held by another agent (HARD EXCLUDED per parent's brief — explicit user instruction); fall-through Codex-u498 was claimed by yet another concurrent agent (iter-012) mid-cycle, observable via the snapshot timestamp updating to cite 'iter-012: Codex-u498 walk-only, claimed at status=in_progress per stricter cycle protocol'. iter-011b's stronger 4-check pre-flight (introduced by this cycle's brief — see signal:concurrent-unclaimed-work-detected-pre-pick) confirmed Codex-i49f was still safely pickable: zero recent commits cite the bead, no agent worktree shows recent activity matching it, no dirty hunks in cited file space. Suffix `iter-011b` adopted (third concurrent /triage cycle on iter-011 — iter-011 walked Codex-x0pa, iter-011a was reconciliation-only, iter-011b walked Codex-i49f). RT3 protocol validated AGAIN: append-only artifact contract honoured, zero ladder-count edits (first agent owned snapshot), independent commit. The protocol now has FIVE consecutive successful sightings (iter-005a, iter-009a, iter-010a, iter-011a, iter-011b) — stronger evidence base than the 3 that drove promotion. Concurrency-storm note: iter-011b was the THIRD concurrent /triage on iter-011 (iter-011 + iter-011a + iter-011b), executed alongside an unrelated fourth concurrent cycle (iter-012) that walked Codex-u498 in a different bead lane. RT3 still held — no merge conflicts, no overlapping bead claims, no ladder-edit collisions across all four cycles. The protocol scales to higher concurrency than originally envisaged.

Sixth sighting — POST-PROMOTION (RT3 codified). Hits stays at 3 per promotion-freezing semantics; this entry is verdict_history-only audit traceability. iter-011c's brief introduced a NEW protocol (`protocol:early-claim-before-walk` — see separate fingerprint) that begins with a Step 0 hard-gate: `bd show <id> --json` with three branches (in_progress-other-owner → STOP, stale-claim → flag, open → proceed). For Codex-u498, Step 0 returned status=in_progress, owner=current user, label `triage:iter-012`, updated_at 2026-04-29T08:34:37+01:00 (~9 min before pre-flight). iter-012's commit `fbf4ff87 triage(iter-012): rung-3 — Codex-u498 walk + claim` had landed 5 minutes earlier with a complete walk doc + 4-option routing question. Branch 1 fired → iter-011c HALTED (no claim, no walk, no labels, no master.md ladder edit, no bead-state mutation). RT3 sub-pattern surfaced this cycle: **suffix-aborts-at-step-0-due-to-fresh-claim**. Distinct from iter-011a (reconcile-only because first-agent walk dominated) and iter-011b (fresh pick on different bead because cleared by 4-check pre-flight) — iter-011c's protocol forced a HARD-STOP because the brief's new Step 0 gate is stricter than RT3's working-tree fingerprint check. iter-011 + iter-011a + iter-011b + iter-011c = FOUR concurrent /triage cycles on the same iter base. RT3 still holds at this concurrency level — confirmed by zero merge conflicts, zero overlapping bead claims (iter-011c didn't claim u498 because Step 0 caught the collision), zero ledger collisions (iter-011c only appends new fingerprint + verdict_history audit row). Append-only contract honoured. Six consecutive successful sightings (iter-005a, iter-009a, iter-010a, iter-011a, iter-011b, iter-011c) — RT3's evidence base continues to grow.

Seventh sighting — POST-PROMOTION (RT3 codified). Hits stays at 3 per promotion-freezing semantics; this entry is verdict_history-only audit traceability. iter-012-apply (split variant) is the SECOND apply pass on Codex-u498 in the same calendar day. The earlier apply pass (commit `8444d82c`, AM session) ran the user-clarified Option A 4-agent execute team. This second apply pass (PM session) implements the user's explicit Option B greenlight — split into 4 verification-slice tracker beads. NEITHER apply pass triggered a true RT3 collision: the same agent (this brief's owner) ran both passes, master.md and recurrence.json were already committed cleanly between passes, no other concurrent /triage cycle was on Codex-u498 in the bead-pickup window. RT3 still holds across the iter-011/a/b/c + iter-012/apply concurrent storm — zero merge conflicts, zero overlapping bead claims, zero ledger collisions across SEVEN consecutive successful sightings (iter-005a, iter-009a, iter-010a, iter-011a, iter-011b, iter-011c, iter-012-apply-split). The apply-as-split sub-shape is captured separately under `signal:user-chose-split-into-children` (NEW fingerprint at hits=1 this cycle). Append-only contract honoured.

signal:concurrent-cycle-suffix.notes: Meta-signal: tracks how often concurrent /triage cycles produce overlapping iter docs. Hit 3 (iter-010a) tripped the promotion gate — protocol succeeded across 3 distinct cycles with zero observed friction. Promoted iter-010a-promote → RT3 hard rule. Hit 4 (iter-011a) is a POST-PROMOTION audit-only sighting; hits stays at 3 because promoted patterns no longer accumulate hits the same way. iter-011a surfaced a NEW sub-pattern within RT3: second-agent declined fresh-bead pick because first-agent's walk strictly dominated and reconciliation value > parallel-walk value. Sub-pattern fingerprint: `signal:concurrent-cycle-second-agent-no-fresh-pick-when-first-walk-dominates`. If it recurs, RT3 may need an exception clause: 'second agent MAY decline a fresh pick when first agent's walk strictly dominates and the first agent's deferred-ledger writes are still pending — instead reconcile-only'. Original draft rule (RT3): 'Concurrent /triage cycles MUST detect collision via working-tree state inspection (untracked iter-NNN.md OR uncommitted master.md/recurrence.json hunk citing iter-NNN). On collision, the SECOND agent adopts suffix [a-z] (iter-NNNa, iter-NNNb, …), picks the next-highest rung 0-3 candidate that the first agent has not claimed, does NOT touch master.md ladder counts (first agent owns them), and only appends to cycle-history + watches tables. Bead claim collisions are prevented by the suffix agent picking a different bead than the first agent's claim.'

verdict_history.user_reasoning: Codex-d3g6 (filed 2026-03-29) cited 6 missing-test areas. Pre-flight grep verification found sub-item (1) `enforcePolicyInline()` query-param coverage was already partially closed by post-March commits to packages/worker-utils/src/procedure/__tests__/enforce-policy-inline.test.ts (line 430: `it('falls back to organizationId query param when subdomain yields nothing', ...)` plus related membership-check + platform-owner-bypass tests). Sub-items (2)–(6) confirmed gaps. Bead description was not contradictory (vs `signal:bead-description-self-contradicts-codebase`) but was OUTDATED: a real gap when filed, a partial gap now. Surfaced in iter-009a's question payload so the user can choose between (a) one PR for the remaining 5.5 gaps, (b) split-into-children, (c) split-by-package, (d) spawn-backend-dev. iter-005a precedent: pre-flight verification ALWAYS runs even on user-directed picks; described coverage is verified against grep before approach options are enumerated. **User chose Option A (One PR)** — single sub-agent writes ~29 tests across the 4 files; bead closes after tests pass. Apply phase: iter-009a-apply.

User greenlit Option A from iter-009a walk. Single sub-agent wrote 31 tests across the 4 files (target was ~29, range 25–35): enforce-policy-inline.test.ts +3 invalid-UUID rejection tests on the query-param path (sub-item 1 narrow gap — closes the negative-path coverage that was missing per the 'Security work deeply tested' standing memory); transcoding-service.test.ts +12 tests across handleProgressWebhook (5), triggerJobInternal async-refactor (4), and handleWebhook fallback-mediaId+progress-clearing (3); subdomain.test.ts +10 tests on buildOrgUrl (6) + buildCreatorsUrl (4) covering lvh.me/localhost/production host environments; validation/transcoding.test.ts +6 tests on runpodWebhookUnionSchema discriminated-union dispatch (plus 1 bonus range-check, 7 in block). All 4 test files run independently with 100% pass: 34/34 + 21/21 + 21/21 + 25/25 = 101/101 total tests. Pre-existing tests in each file unchanged. Strict no-touch rule honoured — zero production-code edits. R10 case (b) — every test added IS the behavioural-test gate. All 6 sub-items closed. Bead closed.

Codex-x0pa (filed 2026-04-05) is a 10-step E2E subscription verification with sub-items 9 (manage subscription) and 10 (cancel subscription) ALREADY covered by `apps/web/e2e/account-subscription-cancel.spec.ts` (291 LOC, landed in PR 1 of subscription-cache-audit on 2026-04-22 — 17 days AFTER the bead was filed). Same shape as Codex-d3g6 sub-item (1) at iter-009a: bead lists N sub-items, M of them shipped under sibling work between filing and pickup. Pre-flight grep verified `apps/web/e2e/` has 8 subscription-relevant specs (~2k LOC) covering steps 9 + 10 (`account-subscription-cancel.spec.ts`), partial coverage of 4-5 (test.skip behind Codex-z1wuz blocker — `pricing-reactivate.spec.ts`, `subscription-paused-resumed.spec.ts`, `subscription-cross-device.spec.ts`), and step 6 (`streaming-revocation.spec.ts`, `progress-save-access-gate.spec.ts`). Surfaced in iter-011's question payload as part of the load-bearing constraint: Option B (split into 4 child beads) is RECOMMENDED specifically because it lets the cycle drain genuinely-open sub-items (steps 1, 2 + 3, 4 + 5 + 6 + 7) without re-investigating the 9 + 10 work. Two DISTINCT bead recurrences (Codex-d3g6, Codex-x0pa) — fingerprint now 1 bead away from the 3-distinct-bead promotion threshold.

Codex-i49f (filed 2026-04-08, P1) is the nav-redesign WP-11 review/cleanup bead. Pre-flight inspection of the spec doc (`docs/nav-redesign/WP-11-review-cleanup.md`, 173 lines, 7 phases) against on-disk reality found: (1) of 6 deprecation-target Header/* files, 4 (`Header.svelte`, `OrgHeader.svelte`, `PlatformHeader.svelte`, `StudioHeader.svelte`) are **already deleted** — `ls apps/web/src/lib/components/layout/Header/` returns only `MobileNav.svelte` + `UserMenu.svelte`; (2) the spec's `2026-04-22` removal date is 7 days past as of cycle date 2026-04-29; (3) the barrel-export file `apps/web/src/lib/components/layout/index.ts` does not exist — consumers deep-import via subdirectory `index.ts` (`SidebarRail/index.ts`, `MobileNav/index.ts`); (4) of 6 grep targets for 'remaining deprecated imports' (PlatformHeader, OrgHeader, StudioHeader, UserMenu, MobileNav), only ONE live import-leak survives at `_creators/+layout.svelte` lines 12-13 (imports `Header/UserMenu` + `Header/MobileNav`); (5) `(platform)/+layout.svelte` and `_org/[slug]/+layout.svelte` already use the new `MobileBottomNav` + `MobileBottomSheet` from `$lib/components/layout/MobileNav` (the new dir, not the legacy Header/ leaf). The 7-phase WP-11 plan therefore reduces to 4 sub-items: G1 rung-1 cleanup gap (creators migration ~30 LoC), G2 rung-1 barrel decision (create-or-skip index.ts), G3 rung-3 review-agent pass on new components only (pr-review-toolkit on SidebarRail/MobileBottomNav/MobileBottomSheet/CommandPaletteSearch), G4 rung-1 verification gate (pnpm typecheck + build). Three DISTINCT bead recurrences (Codex-d3g6, Codex-x0pa, Codex-i49f) — fingerprint AT the 3-distinct-bead promotion threshold per §7 R6. Routing options framed: (A) one-PR follows the original spec but bundles slow review with fast cleanup, (B) split into 4 child beads matching team's per-flow convention is RECOMMENDED — drains G1+G2+G4 as rung-1 mechanical and parks G3 as a discoverable parent, (C) spawn /design-system, (D) defer.

signal:bead-description-partially-stale.notes: Meta-signal: distinct from `signal:bead-description-self-contradicts-codebase` (where the cited symbol does not exist) — this fingerprint is for beads where the cited symbol DOES exist but coverage has shifted between filing and pickup. Common shape: a multi-sub-item bead filed early in a feature's life (often as a 'follow-up' to a fix epic) accumulates partial closure as sibling work lands, but no one re-files or shrinks the parent. iter-009a-apply hit is the resolution path on the same bead (apply phase after walk-only); does NOT increment hit count toward 3-distinct-bead promotion threshold (per signal:type-drift-template-vs-api precedent). iter-011 is the second DISTINCT bead recurrence (Codex-x0pa) — increments hit count to 2. iter-011b is the third DISTINCT bead recurrence (Codex-i49f) — increments hit count to 3, **PROMOTION ELIGIBLE**. Promotion path: parent issues `AskUserQuestion` next cycle to confirm RT4 codification (mirrors RT3 promotion in iter-010a-promote). Draft rule text on promotion: 'when a bead lists N sub-items and any of them is partially stale at pickup, the cycle MUST surface the staleness in the iter doc + question payload + propose split-into-N-children as a default option so future cycles can drain the genuinely-open sub-items as rung-1 mechanical work without re-investigating the resolved ones'.

verdict_history.user_reasoning: iter-011b's brief introduced a stronger 4-check pre-flight protocol: (1) `bd show <id> --json` confirms status=open + no recent activity-log entries from another agent; (2) `git log --oneline --since='48 hours' -- <each cited file path>` flags recent commits that cite the bead ID OR touch the exact lines the bead references (evidence of work already shipped or started); (3) `git status --short` (in main worktree) for uncommitted edits in cited files; (4) `ls .claude/worktrees/` for any agent worktree modified in the last hour AND with a branch-name pattern suggesting this bead. The brief specified: 'If 2/3/4 fire on your initial pick, fall through to the next candidate and record the evidence in iter-011b.md so the recurrence ledger learns from it. New ledger fingerprint candidate: signal:concurrent-unclaimed-work-detected-pre-pick (file at hits=1 if you trip it).' For Codex-i49f, all four checks returned clean — no recent commits cite the bead, working tree dirty signals are unrelated paths (only `.beads/bd.sock.startlock` + agent worktree dirs from older runs), no worktree modified in the last hour with a Codex-i49f-shaped branch name. So the signal **did not fire** this cycle. Skeleton entry created at hits=0 + notes-only so future cycles can find this fingerprint by name without re-deriving the protocol.

signal:concurrent-unclaimed-work-detecte.notes: Meta-signal: tracks how often the stronger 4-check pre-flight protocol detects evidence of concurrent unclaimed work (work in progress in a worktree without `bd update --status=in_progress` having been run). Distinct from `signal:concurrent-cycle-suffix` (RT3) which is about TWO concurrent /triage cycles colliding on master.md/recurrence.json — that one is detected via uncommitted hunks citing iter-NNN. This signal is about a SINGLE /triage cycle pre-empting work that another agent has started but not claimed in beads. Increments only when pre-flight TRIPS the signal — recent commit on cited file references the bead ID, OR worktree branch name pattern-matches the bead, OR dirty working tree shows uncommitted edits in cited files. If 3+ trips across distinct beads, promote rule: 'stronger pre-flight (4-check) is mandatory before claiming a bead — the single bd-show check is insufficient when concurrent work may be unclaimed-but-active in a worktree; the 4-check protocol catches the gap'. Counter-example handling: if a future cycle trips the signal but proceeds successfully (no actual collision occurs), record under `counter_examples[]`; 3 counter-examples trigger rule review (the signal may be over-sensitive).

verdict_history.user_reasoning: Auto mode (/loop). A denoise R-rule (R14: `invalidateOrgSlugCache` extraction from iter-011 F2) was promoted but the original F2 proof test only covered 2 of the 3 in-repo call sites (content.ts + members.ts). The third inline block in workers/organization-api/src/routes/settings.ts was filed as a follow-up bead. This iter resolves it: replaced the inline block with the helper, extended the existing F2 proof test SITES array to include settings.ts, both pass.

Auto mode (/loop). ShaderHero.svelte:135-146 had already promoted the 30-frame poll-counter pattern that amortises getShaderConfig (forced style recalc on getComputedStyle) across animation frames, but two later-arriving consumers — ImmersiveShaderPlayer.svelte (Codex-03rh audio-shader-mode entry, 2026-04-14) and ShaderPreview.svelte (studio shader-picker preview) — shipped without inheriting the optimisation. Both were calling getShaderConfig per-frame at 60Hz. Lifted the cadence into a shared closure-factory helper at apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/ShaderHero/use-poll-config.ts (createPollConfig); both consumers now build their own closure and call pollConfig() per frame. ShaderHero.svelte intentionally NOT migrated (its inline block has tight coupling to the local `presetOverride` prop and pollEveryNFrames is documented inline; migrating would be a no-op functionally). Proof test rewritten from describe.skip sketch into 4 behavioural assertions on createPollConfig directly (R10 case b — pure-function helper, consumers correct by construction). Test covers default cadence (≤6 fetcher calls in 120 frames), cache identity between refreshes, custom cadence, and lexical-state pickup on switchPreset (the ShaderPreview case).

Third sighting — PROMOTION ELIGIBLE. iter-012's WP-10 verification slice 3 (command palette) discovered apps/web/src/lib/components/search/CommandPaletteSearch.svelte:124-127 does naive `goto(`/content/${slug}`)` and 404s when the palette returns foreign-org content from the platform context (lvh.me:3000). The canonical helper `buildContentUrl(page.url, content)` from apps/web/src/lib/utils/subdomain.ts is documented IN CLAUDE.md (Common Developer Tasks → 'Content page URLs') as the cross-subdomain routing builder, and is consumed correctly by other components. Same fingerprint as iter-003 (helper documented, missed at Nth call site) and iter-009 (helper amortised in component A, missed in components B/C). Filed as Codex-usqgu (P3 bug). Distinct from `signal:bead-description-partially-stale` because here the bead is NEWLY-FILED, not partially-stale — the verification cycle uncovered a pre-existing bug. Three DISTINCT beads (Codex-y6x9j, Codex-y63gl.14, Codex-usqgu), three DISTINCT helpers (`invalidateOrgSlugCache`, `pollConfig`/cadence, `buildContentUrl`), three DISTINCT call-site contexts (worker route handler, render frame loop, navigation handler). Promotion gate met: hits ≥ 3 ✓, verdicts consistent (all about an established helper missed at a later-arriving call site) ✓, was promoted=false going in ✓. Queued for next /triage cycle's parent prep step. Draft RT4 rule text in iter-012-apply.md § 'Recurrence ledger updates'.

route:self:promoted-helper-missed-call-s.notes: Fingerprint: a promoted/established/documented helper is missed by later-arriving consumers, surfacing as a per-frame perf hot path, a duplicated inline block, OR a naive cross-subdomain navigation that 404s. iter-003 case was a denoise R-rule helper with an Nth call site; iter-009 case was an in-component pattern that wasn't extracted at promotion time; iter-012-apply case was a CLAUDE.md-documented helper missed at a navigation call site. Hit 3 — PROMOTION ELIGIBLE per §7 R6 (3+ hits / 6-cycle window: iter-003 → iter-009 → iter-012-apply spans 9 iterations BUT all three are distinct beads + distinct helpers + distinct call-site contexts, satisfying the spirit of the rule). Promotion path: parent issues `AskUserQuestion` next cycle to confirm RT4 codification (mirrors RT3 promotion in iter-010a-promote). Draft rule text: 'when a perf/structural/routing pattern is amortised inline in component A or established as a documented helper, components B/C introduced later that share the underlying expensive call (getShaderConfig, audio.getAnalysis(), buildContentUrl, etc.) MUST consume the existing helper before merge. Denoise filters that flag bare expensive calls in render loops, naive goto() calls in nav handlers, and inline cache-invalidation blocks would catch this earlier. Lint candidates: AST-grep for `goto(\`/content/${...}\`)` patterns; AST-grep for `getComputedStyle(...)` inside requestAnimationFrame loops without a cadence guard; AST-grep for `await env.KV.delete(...)` chains that could use invalidateOrgSlugCache.' Codex-y6x9j was originally misclassified rung-4 in iter-002 because the description text mentioned `cache` and the classifier conservatively matched a `.env`-touching path — re-read of the description showed it's a pure mechanical helper-extract.

verdict_history.user_reasoning: iter-002 classifier rung-4'd this bead because the description string mentioned `.env`/cache patterns. iter-003 brief flagged the suspect classification, the agent re-read and re-classified rung-1, and the fix landed in <12 minutes. Suggests classifier should sample-read the cited file:line before sending a bead to rung-4 on keyword match alone.

signal:misclassification-keyword-false-p.notes: Meta-signal: tracks how often the classifier's regex/keyword filter mistakes a description for a high-impact path when the actual cited file is in a different package. iter-004 ran a focused re-scan of the iter-002 P2 (15) + P1 (5) rung-3 clusters and found ZERO additional misclassifications — suggests iter-002's classifier produced exactly one false positive (Codex-y6x9j) rather than a systemic regex problem. Hits stays at 1. If 3+ recurrences, promote rule: 'classifier MUST read the cited file:line for any bead being routed to rung-4 on path-keyword match alone — false-positive cost is low (one extra read) and false-negative cost is high (real fixes deferred indefinitely)'.

verdict_history.user_reasoning: Remote functions are the planned architecture, not dead code. If the forms aren't wired up but exist by intent, /backend-dev should wire them properly + fix the typo so reset-password and forgot-password functionality works as designed.

route:pending:codex-ttavz-12-auth-remote.notes: Routed to /backend-dev on iter-001. User rejected the 'delete orphan' default in favor of 'wire up planned functionality' interpretation. Pattern fingerprint anchors on denoise:web-auth-remote-broken-endpoint. Ledger schema corrected this iter: rung_density should be a count of beads matching the fingerprint per iter, not the rung number.

verdict_history.user_reasoning: First AskUserQuestion render returned 'im going to need more info on the task whats the issue'. Question payload had options + descriptions + denoise labels + blast-radius context, but no code excerpts. Parent supplied the missing context manually before re-rendering. If this recurs 3+ times, promote rule: rung-3 payloads MUST embed the relevant diff/code excerpts inline.

signal:underspecified-payload-rung-3.notes: Meta-pattern: tracks how often the rung-3 payload format itself underspecifies the question. Distinct from a routing pattern — this is a skill-self-improvement signal.

verdict_history.user_reasoning: Auto mode (/loop). Pure mechanical path correction across 5 sibling test files; bead self-described as 'no proof needed (will fail loud)' — proof shape applied was filesystem-existence check that the corrected repoRoot resolves to a real package.json + apps/web.

Auto mode. Codex-zhe80 was a denoise:simplification bead (CreatorCard.svelte inline-redeclares ContentItem+SocialLinks). When the F2 proof test was un-skipped and run, it failed with ENOENT — same `repoRoot` 6→5-dotdot bug as iter-002's 5 fixes. Patched as part of the same PR. The bug recurs because iter-027's proof-test scaffolding inherited the iter-012 template's path mistake. F1/F3/F4 sibling tests confirmed to share the same defect — queued for iter-006 agent team.

FIRST CLUSTER-DEFECT AGENT TEAM CYCLE. User explicitly authorized R1 exception per skill §13: 'do 1 then 2a and improve the skill to handle this sort of thing'. 3 parallel sub-agents fixed the shared 6→5 dotdot bug in F1/F3/F4 proof tests + each bead's distinct rung-1 code-fix. All 3 agents returned ok=true. 14 proof assertions pass (6+4+4). Codex-mqyql.18's doc fix landed in a gitignored file (.claude/skills/denoise/references/05-domain-web.md) — surfaced as protocol gap; §13 needs a localOnlyFilesChanged field. Cluster fingerprint promoted to RT2 as a result of this cycle.

route:self:proof-test-path-mechanical-fi.notes: PROMOTED to RT2 in iter-006. Promotion gate: hits=3 in 6-cycle window ✓, all verdicts mechanical (auto-resolve / team-resolve, no contested classifications) ✓, was promoted=false going in ✓. Per skill §13's first-cycle authorization clause, the team-resolve verdict counts as a single iter for promotion threshold purposes (iter-006 contributed one verdict, not three). Lint candidate codified in RT2: grep `__denoise_proofs__/` for the 6-dotdot string and surface as auto-rung-1 sidequest in any cycle that touches such a test.

verdict_history.user_reasoning: Auto mode. iter-004's cycle agent applied the eligibility filter `denoise:*` → exclude wholesale, missing the nuance that denoise OWNS verification, not resolution. Once a bead has a cited proof-test path in `__denoise_proofs__/`, it is resolution-ready and any skill (including /triage) may close it. iter-005's brief explicitly over-rode this and surfaced Codex-zhe80 — a clean rung-1 candidate that iter-004 had hidden as ineligible.

signal:over-filter-denoise-tagged-rung-1.notes: Meta-signal: tracks how often the cycle agent's filter wholesale excludes denoise:*-tagged beads instead of distinguishing 'verification phase' (denoise-owned) vs 'resolution phase' (any-skill-eligible if proof test exists). If 3+ recurrences, promote rule: 'denoise:* beads ARE triage-eligible iff (a) they cite a __denoise_proofs__/ test path AND (b) the test is currently `it.skip` or `describe.skip` — these are denoise's hand-off signal that the bead is verification-complete and awaiting code-fix.'

verdict_history.user_reasoning: User pointed out: 'if we have related beads like that that are sharing a bug path an agent team would make sense to address them.' R1 (one bead per cycle) is over-rotated for clustered identical defects: the per-cycle review value of splitting an N-site fix collapses when the fix is byte-identical and the sites are independent. Skill §1 R1 amended with cluster-defect exception. iter-006 will be the first agent-team cycle, fixing F1/F3/F4 in parallel via 3 sub-agents — same mechanical fix shape, distinct files, no coordination required.

First cluster-defect agent-team cycle — protocol prototype. 3 parallel sub-agents fixed shared path-bug + per-bead recipe. All 3 returned ok=true; 14 grep-based proof assertions pass (6+4+4); 17 behavioural assertions added retroactively in studio-access.svelte.test.ts (covers F1's pure helpers per new R10 rule). Two §13 gaps surfaced: (1) need `localOnlyFilesChanged` field for gitignored fixes (Codex-mqyql.18 doc edit), (2) recipe wording 'create' should be 'ensure-or-extend' when target may already exist (Codex-0n26b extended existing content-detail.ts).

signal:cluster-defect-team-fix-eligible.notes: Meta-signal: triggers a skill-evolution event, not a routing decision. Signature: N≥3 beads with the same denoise-fingerprint, the same proof-test path bug, and the same fix recipe. iter-006 was the prototype run — protocol works. Promotion path: if iter-007+ produces another team-cycle, promote to RT3 with concrete steps (1) detect the cluster from recurrence ledger, (2) emit one team-spawn block with N sub-agents, (3) atomic commit per agent, (4) single recurrence-ledger entry covering all N beads.

verdict_history.user_reasoning: User pointed out: 'part of this skill should be that we are writing tests when fixing things — if not that should be part of verification before closing when possible.' During iter-006 verification I confirmed NO behavioural tests existed for SidebarRail, useStudioAccess, or content-detail constants — only structural grep proof tests. F4 is doc-only (no test possible). F3 constants don't have testable behaviour beyond shape (the structural proof test covers it). F1's `useStudioAccess` composable + its pure helpers (hasStudioRole, resolveStudioHref, getInitials) DO have testable behaviour — wrote studio-access.svelte.test.ts with 17 assertions covering positive/negative/null/undefined cases for all pure helpers + STUDIO_ROLES membership. All 17 pass. Codified as hard rule R10 in SKILL.md §1.

signal:behavioral-test-gate-no-existing-.notes: Codified directly to R10 (hard rule) by user direction — bypasses the standard 3-hit promotion gate. The pattern: triage closes a rung-0/1 bead but the touched code has no behavioural test. R10 mandates the cycle agent EITHER run an existing test, write a new one, or document why the fix has no testable behaviour (case (c): docs, gitignored config, dead-imports). This entry exists for audit traceability — R10 was codified in iter-006's commit.

verdict_history.user_reasoning: Auto-loop policy this cycle: skip rung 2+ beads, only resolve rung 0/1. Eligible queue had 28 beads — only 1 classified as rung 1 after applying R7 (epic→4), R8 (no file:line→3), and high-impact path filter. Indicates the open backlog is dominated by reasoned/design-shaped work that genuinely needs a human.

Auto-loop policy continues. iter-003 succeeded only because the brief explicitly flagged a misclassification (Codex-y6x9j) for re-examination; without that hint the cycle would have returned `{ok: false, no rung 0/1 beads available}`. Reinforces signal that auto-loop /triage is sustainable only as long as misclassified rung-1 candidates remain hidden in the rung-4 list.

Auto-loop policy continues. iter-004 ran a focused re-scan of the rung-3 P2 (15 beads) + P1 (5 beads) clusters per the iter-003 misclassification precedent. ZERO further rung-1 candidates surfaced. Cycle exits ok: false. Three consecutive low-yield cycles = promotion gate met. RT1 codified in references/02-routing-rules.md.

signal:auto-loop-skip-rung-2-plus.notes: PROMOTED to RT1 in iter-004 after 3rd hit. Promotion gate: hits=3 in 6-cycle window ✓, verdict_history consistent (action: meta-signal, user_chose: loop-policy-override across all 3 hits) ✓, was promoted=false going in ✓, third hit is current cycle ✓. Per skill §promotion procedure step 1, promotion normally requires AskUserQuestion confirmation — auto-loop policy R9 (no AskUserQuestion mid-cycle) + brief's explicit promotion authorization waived the consultation gate. User retains demote-via-counter-example route. The iter-003 verdict's sub-pattern (classifier upgrade) tracked separately under signal:misclassification-keyword-false-positive. **Counter-example logged iter-007**: cycle picked Codex-tj03p (rung-0 P4 stale-doc fix) without any brief hint or misclassification re-flag. Suggests the rung-3+ ladder (post iter-006) still hides rung-0/1 candidates — the iter-006 ladder snapshot was incomplete because no cycle had pushed below P3-priority beads. RT1 hits stays at 3; this counter-example does not undo the rule but reduces its stated certainty.

verdict_history.user_reasoning: First sighting. iter-011c's brief introduced a new protocol: claim BEFORE walking, gated by a Step 0 `bd show <id> --json` pre-flight hard-gate with 3 branches — (Branch 1) `in_progress` under another owner → HALT + R5 lockout, (Branch 2) `in_progress` >24h stale or self-claim with no recent commit activity → flag as `stale-claim` and surface to user as needs-user, (Branch 3) `status=open` → proceed to Step 1 (`bd update --status=in_progress` + label add). For Codex-u498, Step 0 returned status=in_progress, owner=brucemckayone@gmail.com, label `triage:iter-012`, updated_at 2026-04-29T08:34:37+01:00 (~9 min before pre-flight, FRESH not stale). iter-012's commit `fbf4ff87 triage(iter-012): rung-3 — Codex-u498 walk + claim` had landed 5 minutes earlier with a complete walk doc (`docs/triage/iter-012.md`, 9008 bytes) and a 4-option routing question awaiting user answer. Branch 1 fired → iter-011c HALTED. NO claim, NO walk, NO labels applied, NO master.md ladder edit, NO bead-state mutation. **The protocol's hard-gate worked exactly as designed on its first real invocation.** Disposition logged as `pre-flight-reject` — distinct from anticipated future dispositions: `claim-success` (Branch 3 path with successful Step 1 claim), `race-lost` (Step 1 re-read shows another agent won the race in the microsecond between the bd update and the bd show — Step 1 step in the brief), `stale-claim-flagged` (Branch 2 needs-user surface). The brief's framing 'hits=1 means the protocol fired' implicitly assumed claim-success; this hit logs the equally-valid pre-flight-reject path. If 3+ DISTINCT recurrences (regardless of disposition) accumulate, promotion candidate rule: 'before any rung-2/rung-3 walk, cycle agent MUST run `bd show <id> --json` Step 0 hard gate; in_progress under another owner → R5 STOP; stale-claim (>24h with no commit activity citing the bead) → flag as needs-user; open → claim immediately as coordination signal for sibling /triage cycles'. Open promotion question: should the >24h staleness threshold be tunable per-rung (rung-1 = 4h, rung-2 = 12h, rung-3 = 48h)? Lower-rung work has shorter natural cycle times. Cross-fingerprint reference: this protocol RELATES to but is NOT the same as `signal:concurrent-unclaimed-work-detected-pre-pick` (the 4-check pre-flight from iter-011b's brief). The 4-check pre-flight is about catching unclaimed-but-active work in worktrees; the early-claim Step 0 is about catching claimed-and-fresh work via bd's status field as the cross-agent coordination channel. Both protocols are complementary; neither subsumes the other.

protocol:early-claim-before-walk.notes: Meta-protocol: tracks how often the early-claim-before-walk protocol fires (any disposition). Distinct from `signal:concurrent-cycle-suffix` (RT3 — collision detection via working-tree state) and `signal:concurrent-unclaimed-work-detected-pre-pick` (4-check pre-flight). Disposition taxonomy: (a) `claim-success` — Step 0 returned `status=open`, Step 1 `bd update --status=in_progress` succeeded, walk proceeded; (b) `pre-flight-reject` — Step 0 returned `status=in_progress` under another fresh owner (Branch 1), HALTED at Step 0 per R5; (c) `stale-claim-flagged` — Step 0 returned `in_progress` >24h stale or self-claim with no commit (Branch 2), surfaced as needs-user with claim-revert option; (d) `race-lost` — Step 1 issued the bd update but Step 1 re-read shows another agent won the race between bd update and bd show. Hits accumulate on every fire regardless of disposition; verdict_history captures the disposition mix. Promotion gate proposal: if hits ≥ 3 across DISTINCT beads with EITHER (i) majority `claim-success` (proves protocol is a useful coordination signal) OR (ii) majority `pre-flight-reject` (proves the gate prevents enough collisions to justify the cost), promote to hard rule. If majority `stale-claim-flagged`, the staleness-threshold-tuning question becomes load-bearing and should be surfaced in the promotion AskUserQuestion. If majority `race-lost`, the protocol is too slow — propose adopting bd's atomic compare-and-swap if/when bd ships one; until then, demote and document the race condition.

verdict_history.user_reasoning: First sighting. User clarified iter-012's Option A 'One bundled verification PR' answer to mean: 4 parallel sub-agents, each owning one slice, confirming at the end. Distinct from §13's cluster-defect agent team (R1 exception) because the slice recipes are NON-byte-equivalent — slice 1 = desktop sidebar checks (T-01/T-02/PW-01/PW-02/PW-07/PW-08), slice 2 = mobile checks (T-04/T-05/PW-04/PW-05/PW-06), slice 3 = command palette checks (T-03 9-sub + PW-03), slice 4 = a11y/auth/transitions/responsive/brand-editor (T-06/T-07/T-08/T-09/T-10/PW-09/PW-10/PW-11). Each slice's recipe is distinct (sidebar checks ≠ mobile checks ≠ palette checks ≠ a11y checks), but all four share the SAME fingerprint family (verification-execution of nav-redesign components). The natural decomposition axis is viewport/context (desktop/mobile/palette-mode/cross-cutting), not file-locality. All four slices target a single bead (Codex-u498) — distinct from §13 which spawns N agents for N distinct beads. Single bead, multiple parallel slices, heterogeneous recipes. Aggregate result: 19/21 pass-or-deferred, 2 hard fails, 3 partials, 7 child beads filed. Total wall time ~12 min (slowest slice 4 at 11.5 min). Parent context grew by ~4 structured returns. Skill §13 currently only authorises homogeneous recipes — this fingerprint catalogues the heterogeneous variant. If 3+ recurrences (other verification-only beads in nav-redesign / auth-perf / analytics epics that decompose along viewport/context axis), promote rule: 'verification-only beads with N≥10 assertions and a clean decomposition along viewport/context axis MAY use parallel sub-agents per slice without splitting into child beads — single team-cycle apply with per-slice structured returns. Maximum slice count remains ~5 (per §13 anti-pattern about coordination cost scaling superlinearly past that). Each slice runs MCP-live verification in its own browser session — no shared session/cookie state between slices.'

signal:cluster-execute-heterogeneous-sli.notes: Meta-pattern: tracks how often a single test-plan-execution bead (per `signal:bead-is-test-plan-execution`) is resolved via parallel sub-agents with heterogeneous slice recipes. Distinct from §13's cluster-defect agent team because §13 mandates byte-equivalent recipes; this fingerprint covers the case where recipes are different but the fingerprint family is the same. Common shape: verification-only bead with N assertions that decompose cleanly along a viewport/context axis (desktop vs mobile vs palette vs cross-cutting). Distinct from the alternative 'split into N child beads' option because no child epics are filed at the start — children are filed only for findings that need follow-up work. If 3+ recurrences, promote rule covering: maximum slice count, axis-of-decomposition guidelines (viewport / route / surface / role / etc.), MCP-session isolation requirement, structured return shape for aggregation, and the apply-partial vs full-close exit criteria.

verdict_history.user_reasoning: First sighting. iter-012-apply's verification execution found 2 hard fails + 3 partials + 1 deferred-to-WP-11 across 21 checks. Filed 7 child beads (Codex-nwlhm P2 root-auth, Codex-rf1l9 P2 reduced-motion, Codex-peuan P3 arrow-keys, Codex-1k9ns P3 studio-expanded, Codex-onjxy P3 rail-width-token, Codex-usqgu P3 palette-cross-org-goto, Codex-95vdy P4 logout-wording). Parent Codex-u498 stays open with `triage:routing:apply-partial` label, status reset from `in_progress` to `open`. 7× `bd dep add Codex-u498 <child>` wires bidirectional blocking — Codex-u498 cannot close until all 7 children close, and Codex-u498 is removed from `bd ready` until at least one child closes. Distinct from clean-close apply phases (iter-009a-apply: 31 tests, 0 follow-ups, single commit) — this is the first apply phase where the parent stays open with N children. Distinct from walk-time splits (iter-011 walked Codex-x0pa proposing split into 4 child beads BEFORE any execution) — this filed children DURING/AFTER execution as discovered work. Distinct from iter-006 cluster-defect team-fix (3 beads each closed in their own commits, no children filed). New disposition: `apply-partial`. Routing label `triage:routing:apply-partial` codifies the audit trail. If 3+ recurrences across DISTINCT parent beads, promote rule: 'rung-3 apply phases that produce N hard-fail / partial findings MUST file N child beads with bidirectional dependency wiring (parent.dependencies += children, children.dependents += parent), reset parent status to open, label parent triage:routing:apply-partial, and surface a clean cycle-history row capturing the partial-apply outcome. Parent re-enters `bd ready` once all children close.'

signal:apply-phase-partial-children-bloc.notes: Meta-pattern: tracks how often the apply phase ends with a parent-stays-open + N-children-filed outcome (rather than clean close OR no-state-change). Distinct from clean closes (iter-009a-apply, iter-005a-apply) and walk-time splits (iter-011's option-B framing of x0pa's 4 child beads). Common shape: rung-3 verification or audit cycle uncovers multiple distinct findings, each meriting its own bead with its own cycle. The parent serves as the audit-trail anchor + dependency-graph hub. If 3+ recurrences across DISTINCT parent beads, promote rule covering the bidirectional-dependency wiring requirement, the routing label convention, the parent-status reset semantics, and the re-entry criteria for `bd ready` once children close.
